question: Who authored the paper 'A Survey of Agentic GraphRAG: From Retrieval-augmented Generation to Graph-native Agents'?
ground_truth: Zihan Chen, Lei Zheng, Di Zhu

question: When was the paper 'A Survey of Agentic GraphRAG: From Retrieval-augmented Generation to Graph-native Agents' published?

What is the main contribution or summary of the paper 'A Survey of Agentic GraphRAG: From Retrieval-augmented Generation to Graph-native Agents'?
ground_truth: <div> This survey provides a comprehensive analysis of Agentic GraphRAG, an emerging paradigm that integrates Graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with Agentic RAG.&nbsp;<span>While large language models know a lot, their knowledge is frozen at training time and they often suffer from hallucinations.

question: Who authored the paper 'Retrieval-Augmented Generation With Large Language Models'?
ground_truth: Dena Abu Laila, Qais Al-Na'amneh, Mahmoud Aljawarneh, Walid Dhifallah, Khaled Maabreh

question: When was the paper 'Retrieval-Augmented Generation With Large Language Models' published?
What categories does the paper 'Retrieval-Augmented Generation With Large Language Models' belong to?
What is the main contribution or summary of the paper 'Retrieval-Augmented Generation With Large Language Models'?
ground_truth: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as a transformative approach for enhanc-ing large language models (LLMs) by integrating external knowledge sources to address limitations in parametric knowledge and factual accuracy.
